# Janus Kinase 2 (JAK2) Consensus ATP-Competitive Pharmacophore & XAI Attribution Benchmark Guide

---

## Executive Summary

Janus Kinase 2 (**JAK2**) is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase playing a central role in cytokine receptor signaling via the **JAK-STAT pathway**. Dysregulation and hyperactivating somatic mutations (such as **JAK2 V617F**) are primary drivers of myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPNs), including myelofibrosis, polycythemia vera, and essential thrombocythemia.

Because clinical JAK2 inhibitors span distinct chemical classes (e.g., pyrrolopyrimidines, aminopyrimidines, imidazopyridazines, and macrocycles), **JAK2 does not possess a single rigid pharmacophore**. However, structural biology and medicinal chemistry have established a **consensus 4-region ATP-competitive pharmacophore model**.

This guide provides a comprehensive breakdown of the consensus JAK2 pharmacophore, illustrates key FDA-approved inhibitor structures, and establishes an **Explainable AI (XAI) auditing framework** to validate whether Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) learn authentic binding physics or dataset shortcut biases.

---

## Consensus 4-Region JAK2 Pharmacophore Architecture

In the ATP-binding cleft of the JAK2 kinase domain (PDB: [3KYR](https://www.rcsb.org/structure/3KYR), [5W1I](https://www.rcsb.org/structure/5W1I)), inhibitors bind in an elongated conformation spanning four distinct functional zones.

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Consensus JAK2 Pharmacophore Model" width="100%" />

```
                       Hydrophobic Region (Zone 2)
                                   ▲
                                   │
                         Aromatic Ring(s)
                             ╱       ╲
                            ╱         ╲
HBA ── [ Hinge Heterocycle ] ── HBD    [ Deep ATP Pocket ] (Zone 3)
         (Zone 1 - Core)   │
                           │
                         Linker
                           │
                     Solvent Tail (Zone 4)
```

---

### Detailed Breakdown of Pharmacophoric Zones

| Zone | Pharmacophoric Region | Key Chemical Features & Motifs | Structural & Biological Function | Expected XAI Importance |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Zone 1** | **Hinge-Binding Motif** *(Core)* | Pyrrolopyrimidine, Pyrazole, Aminopyrimidine, Imidazopyridazine, Triazine | Forms **1–2 key H-bonds** with backbone amide NH/carbonyl of hinge residues (e.g., Leu932, Glu930). Essential for catalytic domain anchoring. | ★★★★★ *(Highest)* |
| **Zone 2** | **Hydrophobic Pocket** | Phenyl, Fluorophenyl, Chlorophenyl, Cyclopentyl, Cyclopropyl | Fits into the lipophilic pocket adjacent to the hinge; forms van der Waals contacts with Val863, Ala880, and Leu983. | ★★★★☆ *(High)* |
| **Zone 3** | **Deep ATP Pocket** | Benzamide, Pyridine, Morpholine, Sulfonamide, Imidazole | Extends into the back pocket of the ATP cleft; contributes secondary H-bonds and electrostatic contacts with Lys882 / Asp994. | ★★★☆☆–★★★★☆ *(Moderate-High)* |
| **Zone 4** | **Solvent-Exposed Tail** | Morpholine, Piperazine, Hydroxyethyl, Dimethylamino, Propanenitrile | Extends toward the solvent boundary. Optimizes aqueous solubility, pharmacokinetics (PK), and selectivity without rigid hinge contacts. | ★☆☆☆☆–★★☆☆☆ *(Low)* |

---

---

## Auditing Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) with XAI

When evaluating atom/bond attributions generated by Explainable AI algorithms (**Integrated Gradients**, **Captum**, **GNNExplainer**, or **Saliency Maps**), comparing atom importance scores against the consensus pharmacophore reveals whether the GNN has learned **true structural activity relationships (SAR)** or **spurious dataset shortcuts**.

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="XAI Attribution Comparison" width="100%" />

### Expected vs. Biased Attribution Benchmarks

| Sub-molecular Region | Physically Grounded GNN (True Binding Physics) | Biased GNN (Dataset Shortcut / Noise) | Diagnostic Interpretation |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Hinge Heterocycle** | **High (4.5 – 5.0)** | **Low (0.5 – 1.5)** | A healthy model prioritizes the hinge donor/acceptor atoms responsible for primary binding enthalpy. |
| **Hydrophobic Core** | **High (3.5 – 4.5)** | **Moderate (1.5 – 2.5)** | A healthy model recognizes lipophilic contacts driving desolvation energy. |
| **Deep ATP Pocket** | **Moderate-High (3.0 – 4.0)** | **Moderate (1.0 – 2.0)** | Back-pocket interactions provide crucial selectivity against off-target kinases. |
| **Solvent-Exposed Tail** | **Low (0.5 – 1.5)** | **High (4.0 – 5.0)** | High tail attribution signals that the GNN is memorizing solubilizing fragments overrepresented in active compounds. |
| **Synthetic Tags / Alkyls** | **Near Zero (< 0.5)** | **High (4.5 – 5.0)** | Excessive focus on terminal methyls or fluorine indicates severe feature-level shortcut bias. |

---

### Key Peer-Reviewed Publications
1. **McCloskey et al. (2019):** *Using Attribution to Decode Binding Mechanism in Neural Network Models for Chemistry.* J. Chem. Inf. Model. | PMID: [31127041](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31127041/) | DOI: [10.1021/acs.jcim.9b00266](https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jcim.9b00266)
2. **Pope et al. (2019):** *Explainability Methods for Graph Convolutional Neural Networks.* IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR) | PDF: [CVPR Open Access](https://openaccess.thecvf.com/content_CVPR_2019/papers/Pope_Explainability_Methods_for_Graph_Convolutional_Neural_Networks_CVPR_2019_paper.pdf)
3. **Ying et al. (2019):** *GNNExplainer: Generating Explanations for Graph Neural Networks.* Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS) | arXiv: [1903.03894](https://arxiv.org/abs/1903.03894)
4. **Williams et al. (2009):** *Discovery of Ruxolitinib (INCB018424), a Potent, Selective, and Orally Bioavailable JAK1/JAK2 Inhibitor.* J. Med. Chem. | PMID: [20363976](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20363976/)
5. **Wu et al. (2018):** *MoleculeNet: A Benchmark for Molecular Machine Learning.* Chem. Sci. | PMID: [29629118](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29629118/) | DOI: [10.1039/c7sc02664a](https://doi.org/10.1039/c7sc02664a)

### Databases & Structure Repositories
- **ChEMBL Target Repository:** [Human JAK2 (CHEMBL1792)](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/chembl/target_report_card/CHEMBL1792/)
- **RCSB Protein Data Bank (PDB Co-crystals):**
  - [3KYR](https://www.rcsb.org/structure/3KYR) - Ruxolitinib bound to JAK2 Kinase Domain
  - [5W1I](https://www.rcsb.org/structure/5W1I) - Fedratinib bound to JAK2 Kinase Domain
  - [6N7A](https://www.rcsb.org/structure/6N7A) - Momelotinib bound to JAK2 Kinase Domain
  - [6VGL](https://www.rcsb.org/structure/6VGL) - Pacritinib bound to JAK2 Kinase Domain
  - [4WO7](https://www.rcsb.org/structure/4WO7) - Baricitinib bound to JAK2 Kinase Domain
- **PubChem Compound Directory:**
  - [Ruxolitinib CID 25126798](https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/compound/25126798)
  - [Fedratinib CID 16722836](https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/compound/16722836)
  - [Momelotinib CID 25062766](https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/compound/25062766)
  - [Pacritinib CID 46216796](https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/compound/46216796)
  - [Baricitinib CID 44205240](https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/compound/44205240)
  - [Gandotinib CID 46213929](https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/compound/46213929)
